venues.name: Gyeongbokgung Palace
venues.description: The most comprehensive and grandest palace built in 1394 as the main Joseon Dynasty palace by its founder King Taejo. Includes Guard Changing Ceremony and National Museum of Korea.

venues.name: Bukchon Hanok Village
venues.description: A charming neighborhood in Seoul filled with traditional Korean houses (hanoks). Narrow alleyways, beautiful architecture, and a glimpse of Korea's rich cultural heritage.

venues.name: Nami Island
venues.description: Nami Island, with a concept of 'Fairy Tale Village and Song Village,' provides various cultural events, concerts, exhibitions and artists a space for creativity in a natural huge Garden. Ferry ride to the island included.

venues.name: Alpaca World
venues.description: Gangwon-do's first experiential zoo, spans 363,636㎡ of forest. Visitors can interact with and feed alpacas in a beautiful natural setting. Home to Korea's first-born alpaca.

venues.name: Alpensia Ski Resort
venues.description: Transfer to Ski Resort, proceed for ski suit and equipment rental shop. Ski suit and equipment are all included, goggle and gloves are optional. Check in Resort.

venues.name: Yongpyeong Balwangsan Mountain Tour Cable Car
venues.description: The longest round trip cable car in Korea, with 100 eight-seater cabins. Departs from Dragon Plaza and runs for 18 minutes. Arrive at Dragon Castle stop at 1,458m above sea level.

venues.name: DMZ Tour
venues.description: The Demilitarized Zone (DMZ) is a weapons-free buffer zone between North and South Korea. Established July 27, 1953. Includes Imjingak, 3rd Tunnel, and observatory visits.

venues.name: Imjingak Peace Gondola
venues.description: The world's first gondola that passes over a civilian restricted zone (the DMZ), connecting Imjingak in the south to a station across the Imjin River. Offers views of historical military sites.

venues.name: Seongsan Ilchulbong Peak
venues.description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site — a dramatic tuff cone rising 182m from the sea. One of Jeju's most breathtaking panoramas, especially at sunrise.

venues.name: Haenyeo Divers Show
venues.description: The Haenyeo Show at Seongsan Ilchulbong showcases Jeju's traditional female divers as they perform and share their culture. A unique look into Jeju's maritime heritage.

venues.name: Aqua Planet Jeju
venues.description: Korea's largest aquarium with a 5.3-million-litre main tank and over 48,000 marine creatures. Daily shows, touch pools, and haenyeo diver performances.

venues.name: Dodu Rainbow Coastal Road
venues.description: A colorful seaside walkway located on the coast of Jeju. Known for its rainbow-colored lights and ocean views, popular photo spot especially at sunset and night.

venues.name: Iho-Taewoo Beach
venues.description: Known for its unique black and white sand and beautiful sunsets. The iconic red and white horse-shaped lighthouses make it a favourite for families and couples.

venues.name: Dongmun Market
venues.description: Jeju's largest and oldest traditional market, established after the colonial period. A foodie haven offering traditional dishes and South Korean street food.

venues.name: O'sulloc Tea Museum
venues.description: Where Korean tea tradition meets modern design. Opened beside the Seogwang Tea Plantation with a gallery, eco rest area, and café with tea-inspired desserts.

venues.name: Camellia Hill
venues.description: A botanical garden famed for its vast camellia collection and seasonal blooms. Scenic paths and picture-perfect photo spots throughout the year.

venues.name: Eco Land Theme Park
venues.description: A nature-themed park in Jeju where visitors ride a small train through forests, lakes, and flower fields. Relaxing and family-friendly.

venues.name: Seongeup Folk Village
venues.description: A 600-year-old living settlement designated Important Folklore Material No. 188. Home to actual residents preserving Jeju's traditional architecture and way of life.

venues.name: Jeju Tangerine Picking
venues.description: Pick fresh tangerines in Jeju's orchards. Enjoy sweet tastings and learn local farming culture. A seasonal, family-friendly outdoor experience.

venues.name: Jeju Nanta Show
venues.description: A 90-minute non-verbal comedy musical mixing drumming, acrobatics, and kitchen chaos. Entirely language-free and fun for all nationalities and ages.

venues.name: Lunafall
venues.description: A massive outdoor night digital theme park — 2.2km forest trail with LED displays, holograms, and interactive art blending nature and technology.

venues.name: Water World Jeju
venues.description: Korea's largest indoor media art park — 12 themed zones blending water features with immersive light, sound, and interactive art. Heated year-round.

venues.name: Jeongbang Waterfall
venues.description: One of Jeju's top three waterfalls and the only waterfall in Asia to fall directly into the ocean.

venues.name: Jusangjeolli Cliff
venues.description: Designated Jeju Natural Monument No. 443 — stunning hexagonal basalt columns rising 30–40m along a 1km coastal stretch.

venues.name: Garden of Morning Calm
venues.description: Autumn at The Garden of Morning Calm is a vibrant display of yellow and red foliage. In winter the annual light festival transforms it into a magical illuminated wonderland.

venues.name: The Painters Performance
venues.description: A new concept art performance combining live drawings with 3D visual effects, exciting dance, and cheerful comedy. Highly praised for its entertainment value and humor.

venues.name: Everland
venues.description: Korea's largest theme park featuring rides, seasonal festivals, and a zoo. A full-day family destination near Seoul.

venues.name: Starfield Library COEX
venues.description: The most beautiful library in Korea, located inside COEX Mall. A towering bookshelf structure that's a favourite photography and cultural spot.

venues.name: Gwangjang Market
venues.description: Seoul's oldest traditional market, famous for its street food stalls including bindaetteok, mayak kimbap, and raw beef. A must for food lovers.

venues.name: Hanbok Experience
venues.description: Traditional Korean costume Hanbok wearing experience — a must before visiting the Royal Palace. Widely available near Gyeongbokgung.

venues.name: Gamcheon Culture Village
venues.description: Known as the 'Machu Picchu of Busan,' where colorful houses cascade down a coastal hillside. Vibrant alleys adorned with murals and sculptures.

venues.name: Songdo Cable Car
venues.description: Busan Air Cruise revitalizes Songdo Beach, Korea's first public beach. 39 cabins including crystal cabins with transparent floors offering stunning coastal views.

venues.name: Haedong Yonggungsa Temple
venues.description: A stunning temple built on the ocean cliffs of Busan, one of the few seaside temples in Korea. Dramatic views and peaceful atmosphere.

venues.name: Haeundae Blueline Park Sky Capsule
venues.description: A scenic capsule train ride along the coastline of Haeundae. Colorful capsule cars offer panoramic ocean views.

venues.name: Byeo Kkot Nongbu (Gochujang Making)
venues.description: Visit a farm to make authentic Korean Gochujang using traditional ingredients. Learn the process, explore Korean food culture, and take home your own chili paste. Bibimbap lunch included.

venues.name: Gangchon Railbike
venues.description: Ride through scenic riverside tracks on a pedal-powered railbike. Tunnels, bridges, and beautiful nature views. Popular for groups and families.

venues.name: Seaside Railbike Yeongjongdo
venues.description: Ride the Yeongjongdo Seaside Railbike for stunning coastal views and a refreshing sea breeze. Pedal through tunnels, cross bridges, and spot wildlife.

venues.name: Aegibong Peace Eco Park
venues.description: Visit where nature and history come together near the DMZ. Enjoy panoramic views, peaceful trails, and exhibits on biodiversity and peace.

venues.name: Luge Riding Ganghwado
venues.description: Experience the thrill of Ganghwado's luge ride, a gravity-powered adventure down winding tracks. No motors needed — slopes and curves add excitement for all ages.

venues.name: Dasan King Jjimjilbang
venues.description: 24-hour Korean bathhouse featuring hot tubs, showers, and traditional saunas. Exchange key for a locker key which also serves as a charge card inside.

venues.name: Bulguksa Temple & Seokguram Grotto
venues.description: UNESCO World Heritage Site. One of Korea's most celebrated Buddhist temples dating back to the 8th century. Adjacent Seokguram Grotto houses a magnificent stone Buddha.

venues.name: Gwangmyeong Cave
venues.description: A former gold mine transformed into an immersive cave experience with art installations, wine tasting, and unique underground exhibitions.

venues.name: Joyangbangjik Cafe
venues.description: A former 1947 cotton factory turned trendy café. Blending history with modern comfort, it offers tea, coffee, and desserts in a nostalgic industrial setting.

venues.name: Haneul Park
venues.description: In autumn, covered with golden silver grass waving in the cool breeze. From the hill, enjoy a wide beautiful view of Seoul under a clear sky.

venues.name: Namsan Park & Seoul Tower
venues.description: The current symbol of Seoul. Namsan Park, Seoul Tower Observatory, and Love Lock Wall. Accessible by cable car with panoramic city views.

venues.name: Seopjikoji
venues.description: Dramatic coastal headland famous for its volcanic cliffs, wide grass fields, and ocean views. Especially popular in spring with yellow canola flowers. Scenic walking paths and lighthouse.

venues.name: Yongyeon Suspension Bridge
venues.description: Scenic suspension bridge spanning Yongyeon Valley near downtown Jeju City. Beautiful views of cliffs and water, especially impressive at night when illuminated.

venues.name: Strawberry Picking
venues.description: Seasonal strawberry picking experience at local farms. Pick fresh strawberries and enjoy tastings. Popular in late winter and spring.

venues.name: Nurimaru APEC House
venues.description: Former APEC Summit venue set on a beautiful coastal island in Haeundae. Offers scenic ocean views and an insight into international diplomacy.

venues.name: War Memorial Museum
venues.description: Showcases Korea's military history from ancient times to the Korean War with immersive exhibits and interactive experiences. Outdoor military weapons exhibitions.

restaurants.name: Imo-ne Jogae-gui
restaurants.description: Warm and nice seafood noodle soup breakfast near Incheon Airport. First stop for arriving groups. ARRIVED IN INCHEON AIRPORT — meet and greet with tour guide.

restaurants.name: Goryeo Samgyetang
restaurants.description: Classic ginseng chicken soup restaurant. One of Korea's most celebrated national dishes — whole chicken stuffed with ginseng, glutinous rice, jujube, and garlic.

restaurants.name: KkoKko Dakgalbi
restaurants.description: Spicy stir-fried chicken (dakgalbi) restaurant near Nami Island. Popular choice for groups visiting the area.

restaurants.name: Byeo Kkot Nongbu
restaurants.description: Farm-to-table restaurant at the Gochujang making farm. Bibimbap lunch included after the farm experience. Traditional Korean ingredients sourced locally.

restaurants.name: Sandeulhae
restaurants.description: Korean dinner venue popular with groups. Offers variety of Korean set menus suitable for inbound tour groups.

restaurants.name: Maechirang (Gamjatang)
restaurants.description: Specializing in Gamjatang — hearty pork bone stew with potatoes. Warming Korean comfort food ideal for evening group dining.

restaurants.name: Myeongnyun Jinsa Galbi
restaurants.description: Korean galbi (short ribs) restaurant in Seoul. Popular for group dinners with premium quality grilled meat.

restaurants.name: Neulbom Black Pork Restaurant
restaurants.description: Jeju's legendary Heuk-Dwaeji (black pork) — grilled at the table, rich and deeply flavourful. One of Jeju's most iconic dining experiences.

restaurants.name: Hanjeongsik
restaurants.description: Traditional Korean full-course meal (Hanjeongsik) with an array of seasonal side dishes. Elegant group dining experience.

restaurants.name: Noryangjin Seafood Market
restaurants.description: Famous wholesale seafood market where you can select live seafood and have it cooked on-site. Unique dining experience for groups.

restaurants.name: Goryeo Samgyetang
restaurants.description: Classic ginseng chicken soup — Korea's national dish. Whole chicken stuffed with rice and ginseng. Pork-free option available.

restaurants.name: Jeju Ttukbaegi
restaurants.description: Traditional Korean claypot cuisine featuring fresh Jeju local ingredients. Warm and hearty lunch option on Jeju Island.

restaurants.name: Chaeum Hanjeongsik
restaurants.description: Hanjeongsik set restaurant offering traditional Korean full-course meals. Suitable for corporate and group dining.

restaurants.name: Sukseongbushim Aging Pork BBQ
restaurants.description: Specializing in aged pork BBQ. Premium grilled meat experience popular with tour groups.

restaurants.name: Olle Market Food Stalls
restaurants.description: Seogwipo Olle Market street foods: black pork skewers, tangerine desserts, and fresh seafood snacks. Ideal for casual group strolls and snacking.

hotels.name: Courtyard by Marriott Seoul Namdaemun
hotels.description: International 4-star hotel in central Seoul near Namdaemun market. Popular with inbound tour groups.

hotels.name: Migliore Hotel Seoul Myeongdong
hotels.description: Local 5-star hotel in Myeongdong, Seoul's premier shopping district. Convenient for shopping and dining.

hotels.name: Novotel Ambassador Seoul Dongdaemun
hotels.description: International 5-star hotel in Dongdaemun, Seoul. Modern amenities and convenient location.

hotels.name: Lotte City Hotel Myeongdong
hotels.description: Located in the heart of Myeongdong. Part of the Lotte group with reliable quality for tour groups.

hotels.name: Royal Hotel Seoul
hotels.description: Classic Seoul hotel with long history. Central location suitable for tour groups throughout the year.

hotels.name: Jeju Maison Glad Hotel
hotels.description: Local 5-star hotel in Jeju City. Premium amenities and Jeju's iconic hospitality.

hotels.name: Jeju Shalom Hotel
hotels.description: 4-star hotel in Jeju offering comfortable accommodations for tour groups.

hotels.name: Jeju Nanta Hotel
hotels.description: 4-star hotel in Jeju. Convenient for island touring with reliable group facilities.

hotels.name: Jeju Sun Hotel
hotels.description: Local 5-star hotel in Jeju City. Popular with larger inbound groups. Features casino facilities.

hotels.name: Alpensia Ski Resort Holiday Inn
hotels.description: Ski-in ski-out resort hotel at Alpensia. Ideal for ski tour packages in Gangwon province.

hotels.name: Yongpyeong Resort
hotels.description: Korea's premier ski resort hotel. Direct access to slopes and mountain facilities.

hotels.name: Lotte Hotel Busan
hotels.description: Flagship Lotte property in Busan. Excellent location and facilities for large tour groups.

hotels.name: Fairfield by Marriott Songdo Busan
hotels.description: 100% ocean view hotel in Songdo Beach area. Modern Marriott brand quality in Busan.

hotels.name: Novotel Ambassador Seoul Yongsan
hotels.description: International 5-star hotel in Yongsan, Seoul. Modern amenities with easy access to transit.

hotels.name: Jeju Shilla Stay Hotel
hotels.description: Shilla Stay brand in Jeju. Reliable quality and comfortable rooms for tour groups.

clients.name: Cruise Travel
clients.notes: Large group specialist — 450 pax Jeju cruise group

clients.name: Cruise Travel
clients.notes: Small group specialist — mothers trip 10 pax

clients.name: Chan Brothers
clients.notes: Regular client — multiple Seoul group tours 25-30 pax

transport.description: Standard group coach with driver and licensed tour guide. Used for groups 25-45 pax.
Mid-size coach for smaller groups with driver and tour guide.
13-seat van for small groups. Includes driver and English-speaking tour guide.
Korea's high-speed rail — Seoul to Busan approximately 2.5 hours. Ideal for multi-city itineraries.
Two coaches for large groups (46-90 pax). Each with dedicated driver and guide.
